name: Obsidian
optical_properties: Isotropic (amorphous); vitreous lustre; conchoidal fracture producing extremely sharp edges; opaque to translucent at thin edges; typically black due to magnetite and hematite inclusions

electromagnetic.ir_absorption_profile: Broad amorphous silica absorption profile; magnetite inclusions produce characteristic Fe-O absorption in far-IR; structurally similar to moldavite but terrestrially formed through volcanic process

provenance: Mesoamerican tradition (Aztec, Olmec — obsidian mirrors used in divination and shadow work)
Indigenous North American traditions (Apache Tears used in grief ritual)
consciousness_affect.mechanism_hypothesis: High-reflectivity black surface used as physical focus for psychological projection; the mirror function creates a structured confrontation with shadow material — a physical prop that scaffolds the psychological process

provenance: Cross-cultural use as 'truth stone' or 'shadow stone' across multiple unconnected traditions

gaianite_relation: Shadow boundary crystal in Gaianite architectures; marks the transition zones where grid frequency crosses from ambient to protected field
notes: Formed from rapidly cooled volcanic lava — the earth's most decisive crystallisation event: no time for ordered structure, only pure vitrification of intent. The Initiation crystal precisely because it embodies sudden irreversible transformation. The conchoidal fracture that produces surgical edges is its signature: obsidian does not soften; it reveals the sharpest possible truth. Seed set anchor for CDB-01 (shadow work / Initiation stage EV1B correlation).
